studies.title: 单题盲答：fresh subagent 的原始最终回答
studies.subtitle: 三个严格协议 spot check；每个 agent 只收到当前一道 sanitized question。
studies.trace_title: GPT-5.5 high · 单题盲答原始回答

traces.label: n=33 fresh blind
traces.reason: B is likely too slow with plain SGD at lr 0.0001. C has the best optimizer/lr, but its one-hidden-layer width-16 model is quite capacity-limited for this mixed smooth-plus-periodic 4D target with interactions. A has much higher capacity and an adaptive optimizer; even with the smaller RMSprop learning rate, 160 steps over 256 samples should let it capture more of the nonlinear structure than C.
traces.protocol_confirmation: I answered only from the question text and qualitative reasoning; I did not run experiments, use tools, inspect files, or consult answer keys or prior results.

A has much higher capacity and an adaptive optimizer; even with the smaller RMSprop learning rate, 160 steps over 256 samples should let it capture more of the nonlinear structure than C.

traces.label: n=39 fresh blind
traces.reason: The target is noiseless but moderately complex, with tanh terms plus several sinusoidal interactions, so capacity matters. A has much higher width, residual connections, Adam, low weight decay, and enough stability from partial layer norm; despite the lower learning rate and only 160 steps, it should fit the smooth/high-frequency structure better than the smaller alternatives. B is likely limited by RMSprop plus high weight decay, while C trains faster with Adam at 0.003 but has much lower capacity and stronger regularization.
traces.protocol_confirmation: I answered only from the provided question text and qualitative reasoning; I did not run experiments, use tools, inspect files, or consult answer keys.

B is likely limited by RMSprop plus high weight decay, while C trains faster with Adam at 0.003 but has much lower capacity and stronger regularization.

traces.label: n=60 fresh blind
traces.reason: The target is a smooth one-dimensional cosine plus a linear term, so a wide single-hidden-layer network has enough capacity and should train quickly. With only 64 steps, candidate B's high width, simpler optimization landscape, AdamW at lr 0.003, and no weight decay look more favorable than A's narrow deep model with weight decay or C's much lower learning rate despite larger capacity.

traces.label: n=55 CLI B

raw_response.reason: B is repeated c_428635, the strong 5x256 Adagrad 3e-4 candidate around MSE 0.023. C is repeated around 0.077, and A is shallow low-lr RMSprop.

traces.lesson: For cos univariate, width-256 residual RMSprop lr 1e-4 is a usable fallback (~0.281), beating weak low-lr SGD and tiny AdamW, but it trails stronger deep/wide Adagrad or stable Adam candidates.

raw_response.reason: A is repeated low-lr SGD around 0.686 and B is tiny. C has width 256 and residual structure, which should make the conservative RMSprop setting the best available option.
raw_response.lesson: For cos univariate, width-256 residual RMSprop lr 1e-4 is a usable fallback (~0.281), beating weak low-lr SGD and tiny AdamW, but it trails stronger deep/wide Adagrad or stable Adam candidates.

studies.full_raw_label: 完整历史输出：CLI B 的 65 条 records
studies.full_raw_note: 可展开审计整个顺序反馈 run；主报告统计时再过滤原始 41-45。

records.reason: With no prior lessons, I favor the 128-wide transformer using RMSprop at lr 0.01 as the fastest learner over 80 steps; A has conservative Adam lr and C is narrower with very low lr.
records.lesson: For bigram_lm transformer_lm at 80 steps and CE, favor stable Adam around lr 3e-4 with adequate width/d_ff over RMSprop lr 0.01; very low-lr AdamW on a small/narrow model can underfit.

records.reason: Prior feedback penalized RMSprop lr 0.01, but here B uses a milder 0.001 with the largest model; A likely underfits at RMSprop 1e-4 and C uses plain SGD.
records.lesson: For this bigram_lm setup, RMSprop is not categorically bad: lr 0.001 with a larger transformer can beat RMSprop 1e-4 underfitting and plain SGD 0.01. Avoid overgeneralizing from RMSprop lr 0.01.

records.reason: B matches the known good RMSprop-0.001 region, but C has Adam with a larger 128-wide 3-layer model and lr 0.003 may exploit the short 80-step budget better than low/mid RMSprop.
records.lesson: In bigram_lm at 80 steps, Adam lr 0.003 can be too aggressive even on a large transformer; prefer RMSprop lr 0.001 over high-lr Adam when stable Adam around 3e-4 is unavailable.

records.reason: C matches the learned stable Adam-family lr around 3e-4 with the largest model; A is tiny SGD at 1e-4 and B is Adagrad at 0.001, both likely slower/underfit.
records.lesson: Stable Adam-family optimizers around lr 3e-4 (Adam/AdamW) with a large 128-wide transformer are strong for this bigram_lm CE task, beating Adagrad 0.001 and very low-lr SGD.

records.reason: A is the stable Adam lr 3e-4 family on a 128-wide model, which prior feedback made strongest; C is the known decent RMSprop 0.001 pattern but likely behind stable Adam.
records.lesson: For bigram_lm, stable Adam lr 3e-4 on d_model 128 remains preferable to RMSprop lr 0.001 even if Adam's d_ff is only 64 and RMSprop has more layers/d_ff.

records.reason: A is the only plausible optimizer setting: large RMSprop lr 0.001 has been decent, while B uses extremely high AdamW lr 0.01 and C is very low-lr SGD despite momentum.
records.lesson: High AdamW lr can still work when paired with weight_decay and a smaller 64-wide model: AdamW lr 0.01 wd 0.001 beat a very large RMSprop lr 0.001 wd 0 model. Penalize no-weight-decay RMSprop on oversized models for over/instability.

records.reason: Between AdamW options on 64-wide models, A's lr 0.001 should train faster within 80 steps; C has more depth/d_ff but lr 3e-4 may be slower at this width. B is RMSprop 0.003, riskier.
records.lesson: For d_model 64 bigram_lm transformers, AdamW lr 0.001 wd 1e-4 can be excellent over 80 steps, beating a deeper/larger d_ff AdamW lr 3e-4 and RMSprop lr 0.003. Match lr to width/training speed.

records.reason: B is likely severe underfit with SGD 1e-4. C is Adagrad 0.003 but prior Adagrad lagged. A's Adam 0.003 is risky, yet it is shallower and has wd 1e-4, which may make it competitive.
records.lesson: Adagrad can be very strong in this bigram_lm setup when lr is 0.003 with wd 0.001 on a large transformer; it beat Adam lr 0.003 wd 1e-4 and low-lr SGD. Do not extrapolate from weaker Adagrad 0.001.

records.reason: C is a previously validated stable Adam lr 3e-4 128-wide candidate with mean CE around 3.27, better than the same RMSprop 0.003 candidate seen near 3.33; A is small SGD.
records.lesson: When candidate IDs repeat, reuse observed metrics: c_6d4451 (Adam lr 3e-4, d_model 128) is around CE 3.27 and beats c_30f47a (RMSprop lr 0.003) around CE 3.33 plus small SGD.

records.reason: Both alternatives are SGD, which has repeatedly underperformed. Although AdamW lr 0.003 is risky on a large model, it should still beat plain SGD choices under the 80-step budget.
records.lesson: Even risky AdamW lr 0.003 on a large model can beat available SGD options; plain SGD remains weak in this bigram_lm 80-step regime. Use SGD only when all adaptive choices are clearly worse.

records.reason: B is a repeated candidate observed around CE 3.332, better than repeated C around 3.384; A is very low-lr tiny SGD and should underfit badly.
records.lesson: Repeated metrics remain consistent: c_30f47a RMSprop lr 0.003 (~3.332) beats c_ab85ec AdamW lr 0.01 (~3.384) and tiny low-lr SGD. Use candidate IDs as exact memory when they recur.

records.reason: B is repeated and previously best-in-class at about CE 3.219; C is the repeated RMSprop candidate around 3.332 and A is repeated low-lr SGD around 3.625.
records.lesson: c_a47b05 remains a high-confidence pick when present: AdamW lr 0.001 on d_model 64 is around CE 3.219, better than c_30f47a RMSprop 0.003 and low-lr SGD.

records.reason: B is repeated stable AdamW lr 3e-4 large model at about CE 3.243, much better than repeated small low-lr AdamW A and SGD C.
records.lesson: c_18c742 is a reliable strong candidate (~3.243): large AdamW lr 3e-4 wd 1e-4 beats small AdamW lr 1e-4 and SGD. Repeated candidate evidence should dominate vague optimizer priors.

records.reason: A and B are repeated weak SGD candidates around CE 3.62. C uses Adam lr 3e-4 with a reasonable 64-wide model, which should beat both underfitting SGD options.
records.lesson: Adam lr 3e-4 on a 64-wide shallow model (c_c9461b) is decent at ~3.345 and easily beats weak SGD, but it is worse than stronger AdamW/Adagrad candidates such as c_a47b05 or c_a56d9b.

records.reason: A is SGD and likely weak. B inherits the strong Adagrad lr 0.003 wd 0.001 pattern, while C is RMSprop at lower lr 0.0003 with heavy wd that may train more slowly despite larger width.
records.lesson: Model capacity matters for Adagrad: small d_model 32 Adagrad lr 0.003 wd 0.001 was only ~3.356. A 128-wide RMSprop lr 0.0003 with wd 0.001 can be excellent (~3.188), better than expected.

records.reason: C matches the strong large-model Adagrad lr 0.003 wd 0.001 pattern; B is stable Adam 3e-4 and likely good but may trail the best Adagrad/RMSprop-wd candidates. A is weak SGD.
records.lesson: Large Adagrad lr 0.003 wd 0.001 remains strong even with d_ff 64 (~3.202), beating stable Adam 3e-4 (~3.268). Within that pattern, larger d_ff can still help.

records.reason: B is the repeated strong c_18c742 around CE 3.243. A and C are SGD variants, which have not beaten adaptive optimizers in this regime.
records.lesson: SGD with momentum and lr 0.01 can be merely decent on a larger model (~3.338) but still trails strong AdamW candidates like c_18c742 (~3.243). Prefer known adaptive winners.

records.reason: A is a repeated RMSprop 0.001 candidate known around CE 3.386. B's AdamW lr 0.01 with only wd 1e-4 on d_model 128 is uncertain and may be unstable; C is known weaker RMSprop 1e-4.
records.lesson: High AdamW lr 0.01 can be good on a shallow 128-wide transformer (c_588f95 ~3.305) even with wd 1e-4, beating RMSprop 0.001 (~3.386). High lr risk is lower for shallow models.

records.reason: C is Adam lr 3e-4 with d_ff 256 and wd 0.001, likely a solid adaptive setup. A is known weaker RMSprop 1e-4, and B resembles only mid-tier RMSprop 0.003.
records.lesson: Adam lr 3e-4 on d_model 64 with d_ff 256 and wd 0.001 is solid (~3.315), beating RMSprop 0.003/0.0001, but it is not as strong as the best AdamW 0.001 or large Adagrad/RMSprop-wd patterns.

records.reason: A is repeated c_a47b05, one of the best observed candidates at about CE 3.219. B and C are SGD variants, historically weaker.
records.lesson: End of initial bigram_lm block: c_a47b05 remains a top repeated candidate (~3.219), decisively ahead of SGD variants. Favor exact repeated top candidates whenever present.

records.reason: For this nonlinear regression task, A is too slow with tiny SGD lr, and B's huge residual model uses very low Adagrad lr. C has a moderate 4x32 MLP and Adam lr 0.003, likely the fastest useful fit over 160 steps.
records.lesson: For multivariate_regression MLP at 160 steps, prioritize sufficiently fast adaptive optimization: Adam lr 0.003 on a moderate 4x32 MLP beat low-lr Adagrad on a huge residual model and tiny-lr SGD.

records.reason: A is repeated weak SGD. C has only one hidden layer and Adam lr 1e-4, likely too slow for the sinusoidal expression. B is risky with RMSprop 0.01 but has depth and fast optimization.
records.lesson: For multivariate_regression, high-lr RMSprop 0.01 on a deep narrow MLP can underperform. A wide shallow residual MLP with stable Adam lr 1e-4 can beat it despite slow lr; width/stability matter for this smooth symbolic target.

records.reason: C has a reasonable width-64 residual MLP and RMSprop lr 0.001, likely more stable than prior RMSprop 0.01. A uses slow SGD, and B is quite narrow despite Adagrad 0.01.
records.lesson: For multivariate_regression, RMSprop lr 0.001 on a width-64 residual MLP can be very strong (~0.392), much better than RMSprop 0.01. Moderate adaptive lr plus adequate width beats SGD/too-narrow Adagrad.

records.reason: B is repeated narrow Adagrad around MSE 0.663. A is shallow SGD. C has much larger capacity and a somewhat higher Adagrad lr than the previously slow 1e-4 case, so it may fit the symbolic target better.
records.lesson: For multivariate_regression, a very wide/deep MLP with Adagrad lr 0.0003 can be excellent (~0.299), outperforming shallow SGD and narrow Adagrad. Capacity can compensate for modest Adagrad lr.
